description_long: This is a streamlined, vintage-style city bus with a long, rounded body and a ribbed metallic lower section. The bus features a dark, likely black or deep blue upper body with large, rectangular side windows arranged in a continuous row, separated by thin pillars. The front end is curved with a prominent windshield and headlight fixtures, while the rear is similarly rounded and smooth. The wheels are set low and partially covered by the body, contributing to the aerodynamic appearance. Entry doors are located near the front, and the bus appears to have a robust metal construction typical of mid-20th-century public transit vehicles. The overall design suggests durability and capacity for urban passenger transport.
description: A vintage, streamlined city bus with a ribbed metallic lower body, large side windows, and a rounded, aerodynamic shape. The bus is primarily dark in color with a robust, classic design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the rounded nose, windshield, and headlight area, with the ribbed metallic lower section visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the long row of rectangular windows, ribbed lower body, and two visible wheels.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the rounded back, rear window, and the continuation of the ribbed metallic design.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a similar window arrangement and body detailing as the other side.
attribute_window_arrangement: A continuous row of large, rectangular windows along each side, evenly spaced and separated by thin vertical pillars, with no significant variation in size or grouping.

license_info.creator_display_name: The Center for Digital Humanities UArizona
